digest: Standard-depth research supports a practical, standards-aligned AI inference energy/carbon reporting approach: measure owned compute where possible, allocate shared/cloud emissions transparently, and label third-party AI API figures as proxy estimates unless vendors disclose energy telemetry.

finalReport.summary: Working conclusion: for a small SaaS or applied AI team, the most defensible approach is a layered estimate, not a claim of exact per-request truth. Use hardware/runtime telemetry for owned inference, cloud carbon dashboards or Cloud Carbon Footprint for cloud allocation, request/token/model/customer metadata for attribution, and SCI/GHG Protocol-style disclosures for boundaries, functional units, grid factors, and uncertainty. Third-party AI APIs should be reported as proxy or attributed estimates, not measured provider-side inference energy, unless the vendor provides explicit energy/carbon telemetry and methodology.

researchMetadata.sourcePolicy: Primary official docs, standards, project methodology pages, and selected research/preprint evidence were preferred; audited verifier verdicts are used as status gates.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Strong NVIDIA DCGM/NVML and Linux powercap/RAPL evidence; AMD ROCm remains weak.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: verified for NVIDIA/Linux, partial for broader hardware coverage
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Owned NVIDIA GPU and Linux CPU/package measurement is feasible; non-NVIDIA GPU claims need more validation.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: AWS and Google dashboards support cloud/account/project/product/region/month carbon reporting; Microsoft and AI request/token dashboard granularity remain unresolved.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: verified for Google; partial for AWS exact details; unresolved for Microsoft
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Use dashboards for cloud-level context, not direct AI inference request telemetry.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: CodeCarbon supports owned compute estimates; Cloud Carbon Footprint supports public-cloud allocation.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Enough evidence to recommend a feasible tool stack with caveats.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Inference energy varies by model/task; batching and KV-cache affect throughput, while direct energy amortization and quantization evidence are weaker.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: verified for model/task variation; partial for batching-to-energy link
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Track model/version, task, token counts, batching/concurrency, latency, and utilization; avoid unsupported quantization energy claims.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: SCI functional unit/boundary disclosure and reserved/provisioned hardware energy inclusion support average allocation; CCF supports cloud operational allocation.
Use explicit grid-intensity choice, region, PUE, and location/market-based accounting distinctions.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Prevents conflating physical grid-intensity estimates with market-based renewable/accounting claims.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: SCI, GHG Protocol Scope 2, ISO 14067, and ISO 14040 provide reporting frames.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: AI inference reports should be framed as disclosed estimates/intensities, not unaudited precise product LCAs unless the scope supports that.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Uncertainty contributors are well identified qualitatively; no robust numeric uncertainty ranges were verified.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Use sensitivity bands and caveats rather than false precision.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: Practical path: combine cloud dashboards/CCF, CodeCarbon or hardware telemetry, request/token/model/customer tags, SCI functional units, and disclosed assumptions.
factSlotCoverage.evidenceStatus: partially supported synthesis from verified components
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Actionable, but should be labeled as a synthesized implementation pattern.

factSlotCoverage.bestValue: OpenAI/Gemini/Anthropic public docs expose usage proxies; fetched snippets did not show provider-side energy/carbon telemetry.
factSlotCoverage.parentImpact: Third-party API reporting must remain proxy-based unless vendors disclose measured data.

mainFindings.finding: Owned infrastructure can be measured or estimated with credible low-level telemetry on NVIDIA/Linux stacks.
Cloud provider carbon dashboards are useful for cloud/account/project/product/region/month allocation context, but are not request/token/model-level AI inference meters.
SCI provides the strongest software-specific reporting frame: define a boundary and functional unit, include provisioned hardware energy, and disclose method and assumptions.
Model/task choice is a major inference-energy driver; batching/KV-cache evidence supports throughput effects, but direct energy amortization evidence is only partial.
For opaque third-party AI APIs, usage/token/request/model data are available proxies, but provider-side energy/carbon telemetry was not found in fetched docs.

recommendations.recommendation: Build reporting as an estimate pipeline with explicit evidence tiers: measured owned hardware, allocated cloud footprint, and proxy third-party API estimates.
recommendations.evidenceStatus: verified components plus partially supported synthesis

recommendations.recommendation: For owned inference, collect request id, customer/tenant, model/version, input/output tokens, latency, batch/concurrency, hardware id, region, GPU power/energy where available, CPU/package energy where relevant, and utilization.
Use SCI-style functional units such as per request, per 1,000 tokens, per completed workflow, or per customer-month, and disclose the boundary and allocation driver.
Use cloud dashboards and Cloud Carbon Footprint for account/project/service allocation and reconciliation, not as direct proof of AI request-level energy.
For OpenAI/Gemini/Anthropic-style APIs, publish proxy estimates only, based on usage metrics and declared assumptions, unless the vendor supplies audited energy/carbon data.

actionPlan.step: Define reporting boundary and functional units before instrumentation.
Instrument owned compute first: DCGM/NVML for NVIDIA GPUs and Linux powercap/RAPL where applicable; validate device support and sampling semantics.
actionPlan.support: NVIDIA DCGM/NVML and Linux powercap docs.

actionPlan.step: Add application-level attribution: request, token, model/version, tenant/customer, batch/concurrency, latency, and region tags.
actionPlan.support: Usage API docs plus runtime-factor evidence.

actionPlan.step: Reconcile monthly cloud emissions with Google/AWS dashboards or CCF and allocate shared/idle/provisioned energy using a documented driver.
actionPlan.support: Google methodology, AWS overview, CCF methodology, SCI.

actionPlan.step: Publish uncertainty notes and avoid numeric precision beyond the source data; include qualitative uncertainty drivers and sensitivity bands where local measurements permit.
actionPlan.support: NVML sampling caveats, CodeCarbon exclusions, CCF and SCI methodology constraints.

caveatedFindings.finding: AWS CCFT details were partially supported but failed a strict evidence-row gate, so use AWS dashboard claims cautiously until the exact primary evidence is re-inspected.
KV-cache/batching affects throughput, but the direct energy amortization implication needs additional energy-specific support.
Third-party API docs showed usage proxies and no fetched energy telemetry, but absence is not exhaustively proven.
The small-SaaS implementation path is a synthesis from supported components, not a single prescriptive source.

unverifiedButRelevant.lead: AMD ROCm SMI power/energy telemetry may be relevant for non-NVIDIA deployments, but strong primary-source snippets were not secured.
unverifiedButRelevant.parentImpact: Could change hardware telemetry recommendations for AMD stacks.

unverifiedButRelevant.lead: Microsoft Emissions Impact Dashboard granularity/methodology was not resolved.
unverifiedButRelevant.parentImpact: Could affect cloud dashboard comparison for Azure users.

unverifiedButRelevant.lead: Quantization may affect energy/throughput, but exact inference-energy evidence was not verified.
unverifiedButRelevant.parentImpact: Should not be used as a quantified reduction lever without local measurement or better sources.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Choose whether the report is for operational engineering, customer-facing sustainability disclosure, or formal product carbon/LCA reporting.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: SCI-style operational intensity is much easier to support than ISO-grade product carbon footprint claims.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Use SCI/GHG language for near-term reporting; reserve ISO/LCA framing for audited or expert-led work.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Decide how to treat third-party AI APIs.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: Public docs support usage proxies, not measured provider inference energy in fetched evidence.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Label API emissions as proxy/attributed estimates and separate them from measured owned-compute telemetry.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Decide allocation driver for shared/idle/provisioned capacity.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: SCI says provisioned hardware energy should be included; allocation can materially shift customer/request intensity.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Pick and disclose a stable driver such as GPU-seconds, token share, request runtime, or customer usage share.

parentDecisionNotes.note: Do not set exact numeric thresholds from this research alone.
parentDecisionNotes.whyItMatters: No robust numeric uncertainty ranges were verified and several quantitative dashboard details are partial.
parentDecisionNotes.suggestedParentDecision: Use local calibration and sensitivity analysis before publishing exact thresholds or targets.

partiallyAnswered: direct proof of no third-party API energy telemetry

blocking.gap: Third-party AI API measured energy/carbon telemetry was not found; absence is inferred rather than explicitly proven by vendors.
blocking.whyItMatters: Blocks claims of measured per-request provider-side AI inference energy for opaque APIs.
blocking.nextStep: Ask vendors for energy/carbon telemetry or methodology; otherwise keep proxy labeling.

blocking.gap: No robust numeric uncertainty ranges were verified.
blocking.whyItMatters: Blocks precise confidence intervals or hard action thresholds.
blocking.nextStep: Run local sensitivity analysis over telemetry sampling, allocation driver, PUE/grid factors, and token/model mix.

nonBlockingCaveats.gap: AMD ROCm primary telemetry evidence weak.
nonBlockingCaveats.whyItMatters: Limits advice for AMD GPU deployments.
nonBlockingCaveats.nextStep: Inspect ROCm SMI/API primary docs before recommending AMD instrumentation.

nonBlockingCaveats.gap: Microsoft cloud emissions dashboard evidence unresolved.
Quantization energy effect lacks exact support.
nonBlockingCaveats.whyItMatters: Avoid presenting quantization as a verified energy-reduction lever without measurement.
nonBlockingCaveats.nextStep: Benchmark locally or find energy-specific inference studies.

nonBlockingCaveats.gap: AWS CCFT exact details partially supported but strict audit left one evidence-operability gap.
nonBlockingCaveats.whyItMatters: Avoid using AWS quantitative dashboard details as verified thresholds.
nonBlockingCaveats.nextStep: Re-inspect the AWS overview source before quoting exact dates/resolution.

claims.supportSummary: DCGM exposes board power and total GPU energy fields.
NVML supports power readings and total energy for supported Volta-or-newer devices; semantics vary by architecture.
claims.caveat: Device support and successful NVML calls required.

claims.supportSummary: Linux powercap exposes energy_uj under Intel RAPL zones.
AWS CCFT dashboard details partially supported but strict evidence gate prevented verification.
claims.caveat: Re-inspect before using exact quantitative/details as verified.

claims.supportSummary: Google reports monthly customer-specific footprint by product/project/region and allocates emissions by usage.
claims.caveat: Methodology/data updates may revise historical values.

claims.supportSummary: CodeCarbon estimates GPU/CPU/RAM electricity and applies regional carbon intensity, with exclusions.
CCF estimates public-cloud energy/emissions using usage, conversion factors, PUE, and grid factors.
claims.caveat: Minor unit wording inconsistency between Wh/kWh references.

claims.supportSummary: Study supports large model/task variation in inference energy/carbon.
claims.caveat: Preprint/source page rather than peer-reviewed journal evidence.

claims.supportSummary: Supports batching/KV-cache memory and throughput issues, not direct energy amortization.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: KV-cache inefficiency can limit batch size and throughput; energy amortization needs separate evidence.

claims.supportSummary: SCI defines carbon intensity per functional unit and requires boundary/methodology disclosure steps.
claims.caveat: Some elements are procedure requirements rather than separate disclosure labels.

claims.supportSummary: SCI supports region-specific grid intensity and excludes market-based measures for connected grid electricity.
claims.caveat: Use SCI exact unit wording gCO2eq/kWh.

claims.supportSummary: SCI says include all energy consumed by reserved/provisioned hardware.
GHG Protocol Scope 2 covers purchased/acquired electricity and market-based quality/disclosure criteria.
claims.caveat: Source wording says measurement/accounting requirements rather than exact phrase corporate accounting.

claims.supportSummary: ISO 14067 and 14040 support product carbon footprint/LCA framing.
claims.caveat: ISO 14040 is principles/framework; ISO 14044 has detailed requirements.

claims.supportSummary: Docs expose usage/token/limit/pricing proxies; fetched evidence did not show energy/carbon telemetry.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Inspected docs expose usage proxies and did not surface energy/carbon telemetry.

claims.supportSummary: Implementation path is a supported synthesis from dashboards, telemetry/tools, usage metrics, and SCI disclosure.
claims.caveat: No single source prescribes this exact small-SaaS stack.
claims.correctionOrCounterclaim: Use dashboards, owned telemetry/tools, usage metrics, and explicit assumptions; label API estimates as proxy-based.
